inlines.text: CentrMark renders an outline from your document using the
directive form: two colons, a space, the directive name, then optional SDL-style properties in square brackets.
), matching how block directives use a space after the colon run (for example
At render time, implementations collect headings (and any other outline nodes they support) and emit a navigable table of contents at the directiveΓÇÖs position.
limits how many heading levels appear in the outline. Smaller values keep the TOC shallow; larger values include deeper
anywhere: top of the page, after an abstract, or mid-document when you want the outline to interrupt the narrative.
This heading exists so the TOC above has real entries to aggregate.
